questions.question: List the four primary assumptions underlying Linear Programming models.
expectedPoints: Proportionality: Contribution of each variable is proportional to its value.
Additivity: Total value is the sum of individual contributions.
Certainty: All parameters (coefficients) are known constants.

questions.question: Formulate an LP model for the following scenario: A workshop makes belts and wallets. A belt requires 2 hours of cutting and 3 hours of stitching, yielding $15 profit. A wallet requires 1 hour of cutting and 4 hours of stitching, yielding $12 profit. There are 50 hours of cutting and 90 hours of stitching available per week. Maximize weekly profit.
expectedPoints: Define decision variables: Let x be number of belts, y be number of wallets.
Present the complete model: Maximize Z = 15x+12y, subject to: 2x+y≤50, 3x+4y≤90, x,y≥0.
